CONTENT.Honours: IFK NorrköpingAllsvenskan: 2015Svenska Supercupen: 2015Djurgårdens IFAllsvenskan: 2019IndividualAllsvenskan Top Scorer: 2015
CONTENT.Club career: Kujović started his career in Superettan and Landskrona BoIS, in 2007 he joined Halmstads BK but was directly loaned out to Falkenbergs FF in Superettan for a year, in 2008 he returned to Halmstads BK and debuted in the first match of the season against Gefle IF (1-0) as a substitute.While at Halmstads BK, he played with his older brother Ajsel Kujović, who represented the club between 2006 and 2009.Kujović was signed by Turkish club Kayserispor in October 2010 on a four-year deal starting in January 2011. On 5 January 2011, he scored his first goal for Kayserispor against Ankaragücü. In 2013, he also played, on loan, three games with the Elazığspor.On 9 August 2013, Kujović signed a 3.5-year contract with IFK Norrköping where he reunited with former Halmstads BK trainer Janne Andersson.On 13 August 2019, he signed a 2.5-year contract with Djurgårdens IF.
CONTENT.Personal life: Kujović is a practicing Muslim of Bosniak descent. He is the younger brother of Ajsel Kujović, who was a professional footballer.
CONTENT.External links: Emir Kujović at National-Football-Teams.comHBK profile at the Wayback Machine (archived 1 April 2008) (in Swedish)Emir Kujović at the Swedish Football Association (in Swedish)
CONTENT.Career statistics: Scores and results list Sweden's goal tally first, score column indicates score after each Kujovic goal.
CONTENT.International career: Kujović debuted for Sweden under-21 team 27 March 2009 against Belgium. He was called up to the senior Sweden squad to face Denmark for the UEFA Euro 2016 play-off in November 2015. Emir Kujovic was a squad player for Sweden at Euro 2016. He has earned a total of 5 caps, scoring 1 goal. His final international was a September 2016 FIFA World Cup qualification match against the Netherlands.

SUMMARY: Emir Kujović (Cyrillic: Емир Кујовић; born 22 June 1988) is Swedish professional footballer who plays as a striker. He has won five caps for the Sweden national team, and was a squad player at UEFA Euro 2016.
